TextENGNew: It was a lazy, quiet afternoon, same as any other.
Since I couldn't just stroll around outside freely, even something as simple as shopping required coordinating alibis with Mion.
changeReason: Old says 'match schedules'; new says 'coordinating alibis.' JPN 口裏を合わせる specifically means coordinating stories/alibis, not schedules.

TextENGNew: (Keeping our stories straight had become second nature by now, and it no longer felt like any kind of burden.)
So, naturally, I always ended up buying everything in bulk.
Today was no different. I'd been making trip after trip between various shops and my apartment, snatching up this and that.
changeReason: Old omits detail present in JPN; new includes 'making trip after trip' and 'snatching up this and that' which match JPN あれやこれやと買い漁っていた.

TextENGNew: ``Lessee, I got that, got this...@k@vS06/06/150600306. Oh, wait!@k@vS06/06/150600307. Isn't today the hundred-yen sale?!@k@vS06/06/150600308. I totally forgot!``
With that thought, I spun on my heel in high spirits, swinging both arms wide.@kLike a little whirlwind.
That whirlwind smacked its arm straight into a motorcycle parked obnoxiously across the sidewalk, blocking the way.
``...Ow!!@k@vS06/06/150600310. Wh... Geez!!@k@vS06/06/150600311. Who parked their bike here?!``
Still riding my good mood, I kicked the offending motorcycle without a second thought.
There were three motorcycles in a row.
They toppled one after another, like dominoes.
It was such a clean chain reaction that even I had to laugh.
When you're in high spirits, your guard tends to slip in all sorts of ways.
I had completely failed to notice that the owners of those bikes were right nearby, and that they looked like seriously bad news.
They grabbed me by the collar as I tried to strut away triumphantly, and dragged me into a dim back alley.
Then came a torrent of abuse...
The scene had shifted so abruptly that for a while, I just stood there blinking, unable to process what was happening...
``Dafuq'd ya jus' do, ya lil'... I'mma freakin'... graahhh!!``
``Yer dead, ya hear me?! Yer freakin' DEAD!!``
``Grraaah!!@k@vS06/00/huryoC15002. Think ya can jus' mess with our stuff an' walk away?!``
...Wow.@kTheir Japanese was impressively broken.
I honestly couldn't make out what they were saying, which almost made me tilt my head and laugh.
Translating the three of them as charitably as possible, they were essentially saying this:
'We, the three of us, are deeply upset that you have knocked over our motorcycles.'
'We would appreciate it if you would acknowledge your wrongdoing, offer an apology, and immediately pay for repairs as well as compensation for emotional distress.'@k...Something like that, probably.
'We have now stated our position. We would be most interested to hear your thoughts on the matter.'
'We would be honored to receive your esteemed opinion, so please, do share your perspective.'@k...That's about the gist of it.
``Graaahh!!@k@vS06/00/huryoA15003. Ya zonin' out?! Don'tcha ignore us!!``
``Ah, I'm sorry.@k@vS06/06/150600313. I'm afraid I've reached the limits of my translation abilities.``
``Ahhh?!@k@vS06/00/huryoA15005. Whatchu say to me?!!!``
Of course, I understood the general idea.
But honestly, parking your bikes right across the sidewalk like that? Someone was bound to deliver divine punishment sooner or later.@kI considered myself the instrument of a higher justice.

Bad manners deserve proper penalties. It wouldn't be fair to those who follow the rules otherwise.@kGranted, mine was technically an accident, but from a cosmic perspective, this was righteous retribution.
But, God...@kYou're free to use me as your tool of justice, but what about the part where I get dragged into a back alley and berated by three thugs? Where's the divine follow-up on that...?
I let out one long, heavy sigh.
...It should go without saying, but@kI didn't feel even a shred of fear in this situation.
Because... I genuinely didn't consider it all that dangerous.
Three large men ganging up on a single girl and screaming at her might have been intimidating in the abstract, but there were far too many disadvantages stacked against them.
First, the difference in gender and numbers.
It goes without saying: even if I were eighty percent in the wrong, a man shouting at a woman automatically becomes the villain.
And with the ratio at three to one, the sheer lopsidedness of force only made them look worse. No matter how fairly you judged it, this could never end in a draw.
Then there was the back-alley setting, and the fact that their demands had escalated beyond an apology to extorting money. Both were fatal mistakes on their part.
With this many strikes against them, public sympathy was firmly on my side.@k...In other words, the outcome of this fight was already decided.
Their only path to victory was to intimidate me into handing over whatever pocket change I had before the court of public opinion could intervene.
...Thinking about it that way, dragging me into this alley where they were less likely to be discovered was an understandable, if crude, tactical choice.
``Huuuh!! Ya listenin' or what?!``
But I already knew their odds of winning were virtually zero.
First of all, they had picked the wrong target.
Right now, I was 'Sonozaki Mion.'
Attacking her in the town of Okinomiya was the height of stupidity.
People connected to Hinamizawa would rally in no time.
And they don't take kindly to outsiders threatening one of their own.
Second, these punks clearly had no familiarity with the area.
If they did, they'd already know what kind of person 'Mion' was, and how suicidally reckless it was to mess with her.
And they wouldn't know that this seemingly deserted alley wasn't as empty as it appeared.
This back street, which looked like nobody ever used it, was actually a well-known shortcut that locals used on their way home at certain hours.
Come evening, it would transform into a well-traveled thoroughfare.
And the time was approaching dusk.
All I had to do was stall, and soon enough the street would fill with foot traffic.@kThey'd have no choice but to scatter.
Neither pushing nor retreating.
A little more patience and the victory was mine.@kIt was that simple a fight.
They hurled abuse and grabbed me by the collar, but they weren't actually hitting me.
Three cheers for compulsory education and its subconscious lesson in 'ladies first.'
...But my fatal flaw was that even in a fight I could win just by waiting, I couldn't resist the urge to play.
I wanted to make them look even more villainous, to back them into an ever-tighter corner where excuses would be impossible.@k...That devilish impulse was perking up its little horns.
Put simply, all I had to do was play the poor, pitiful victim even more convincingly.
The kind of tragic, heartbreaking heroine that makes everyone want to rush to her side.
...In situations like these, bursting into tears immediately makes men flinch and pull back.
That would work for crisis avoidance, but my goal wasn't to escape. It was to create a quagmire. So that approach was off the table.
changeReason: Old vaguely says 'I needed something different here'; new specifies 'my goal wasn't to escape. It was to create a quagmire.' JPN 泥沼化 (quagmire) is the explicit goal stated.

TextENGNew: I'd start defiant, feigning bravado, a little provocative.
...But gradually, the strength would drain from my voice. I'd start to waver, to tremble, my eyes glistening with tears...
...Around that point, if I could slip in just the right hint of that irresistible quality -- the strong girl slowly crumbling -- it would be even more effective.
...Even if you understood the theory, actually pulling it off? Probably only Mion and I could manage that.@k...Though of course, I'd do it leagues better than her.
``...Wh-Why... do you have to keep hounding me like this?!@k@vS06/06/150600315. I said I was sorry, didn't I...?``
A hint of defiance still peeking through, but underneath, unmistakable nervousness.
And before I knew it, a thin film of tears in my eyes.@k...How's that for a performance?
Come to think of it, I've heard that men can't control their tear ducts at will.@kWhat clumsy creatures.
My sudden shift in tone threw the trio off balance for a moment, but they quickly recovered and resumed pelting me with their incomprehensible tirade.
Gradually deeper... gradually further in...
Dragging them into a tableau that anyone would recognize as unmistakable bullying, beyond any possible excuse...
``Wh-What's yer problem?!@k@vS06/00/huryoA15008. Where'd all that attitude go, huh?!``
``...But I... don't... *hic*... *hic*... aaauuuhhh...``
``...If yer gonna cry like that... ya shoulda quit actin' tough from the start...!``
Oh dear, the trio was losing steam.@k...Had I laid it on a bit too thick?
It'd be so boring if they just let me off with a 'fine, we'll let you go this time'...
...Just as I was thinking that--
``Th-That's... enough...!@k@vS06/07/151200002. Can't you see she's crying?! Stop it...!!``
He was a boy around my age.
Slender build, slight for a boy.
Delicate -- that was the word anyone would reach for as a first impression.
His slight frame and fine features made him look younger than he probably was.
But his sharp, intelligent eyes were wide open, unflinching even in the face of what could only be called reckless bravery.
``What da hell...?!@k@vS06/00/huryoA15010. Who the hell are you?!!``
``Mind yer own business before we mess you up too, raaaghh!!!``
Apparently deciding that a bookish-looking boy would be easier to deal with than a crying girl, the three abandoned me and turned their fury on him.
He did flinch a little as they snarled at him one after another.
Nobody could stay completely unfazed under that kind of aggressive hostility.
...But he had come here prepared for this.
He had known full well that speaking up to save me would inevitably lead to this exact situation, and he'd stepped forward anyway.
``Can'tcha hear?!@k@vS06/00/huryoA15012. Answer us, dammit!!``
He must have wanted to shout back at them with fierce words of his own.
...But in all his life up to that point, he had never traded this kind of ugly language with anyone.
So he couldn't put the surge of emotion into words.
No -- he couldn't speak because he didn't even know what words to use.
All he could do was speak with his eyes.
To convey, through his gaze alone, his unshakable resolve to stand his ground.
To the three thugs, it probably looked like the boy in front of them had gone mute with terror.
But soon enough, they would understand.
changeReason: Old says 'But I knew different' (narrator's knowledge); new says 'But soon enough, they would understand' (thugs' future realization). JPN やがてわかる means 'eventually they'll understand.'

TextENGNew: ...If sheer force of will could settle a fight, then these three had already lost to his.
...And lose they did.
Overpowered by his resolve, backed into a corner, they had no recourse left but brute force.
They grabbed him, punched him, kicked him, threw him to the ground.
...It was painful for him, certainly. But I, who had watched the entire thing, knew the truth.
He had won. Without question.
Eventually, just as I'd planned, passersby arrived and condemned the thugs' brutality.
changeReason: Old says 'local passersby stopped the punks'; new adds 'just as I'd planned.' JPN 私が望んだとおりの頃合に means 'at the timing I had hoped for.'

TextENGNew: The three finally realized there was nothing more to gain by staying, and fled in a panic.
...He stood up, brushing the dust from his clothes.
When our eyes met, he gave me the faintest, wryest smile and asked, 'Are you all right?'
...He was the one who should have been asked that.
``...You really do like picking fights you can't win, don't you?@k@vS06/06/150600321. Next time, I'd strongly recommend making sure you can actually win before charging in.``
``...I did hesitate at first.@k@vS06/07/151200005. Ahahahaha...``
He laughed sheepishly.@k...He had been watching.@kFrom the beginning.
...Three thugs, each terrifying enough on his own.@kHe'd had every opportunity to look the other way and walk on by.
changeReason: New adds 'Three thugs, each terrifying enough on his own' from JPN 一対一でだって恐ろしいようなチンピラ崩れの不良が３人も, which old version omitted.

TextENGNew: ...And yet he rejected that temptation and threw himself into the fray.
``That's not called courage -- it's called recklessness.@k@vS06/06/150600323. Or maybe just not knowing your own limits?``
``...Mmph.@k@vS06/07/151200007. You never pull your punches, do you, Mion...@k@vS06/07/151200008. You could at least say 'thank you for saving me.'@k@vS06/07/151200009. You were crying, you know.``
``...I-I was not crying!@k@vS06/06/150600325. That was acting...!``
``Ahahahahahaha.@k@vS06/07/151200011. Sure, sure. Acting.@k@vS06/07/151200012. Ahahahahaha.``
``H-Hey!@k@vS06/06/150600327. You don't believe me, do you?!@k@vS06/06/150600328. Do you honestly think I'd actually cry?!``
``Of course not.@k@vS06/07/151200014. Nobody would ever believe that the great and fearless Sonozaki 'Mion' was brought to tears by a few punks. Ah ha ha ha.``
``Wh-What are you laughing at?!@k@vS06/06/150600330. You really don't believe it was an act?!@k@vS06/06/150600331. There's no way I'd ever actually cry!!``
``Ha ha ha ha. I believe you, I believe you.``
Through that exchange, I quietly realized something.
...He was one of Sis's friends. One of Sonozaki Mion's friends.
And he didn't know that Mion had a twin sister.@kHe thought I was Mion with her hair down.
...Our family had separated us before elementary school.
Mion went to Hinamizawa.
I went to Okinomiya.
Everything -- school, environment, daily life -- was kept completely separate from Hinamizawa.
So it wasn't strange for someone not to know I existed.@kIn fact, people who did know were the rare exception.
The words were right there on the tip of my tongue -- 'I'm not Mion, I'm her younger sister, Shion' -- but I swallowed them just in time.
...Shion wasn't supposed to be here.
He was from Hinamizawa.@kIf he found out that someone who looked exactly like Mion was living in town... that would be a problem...
So without hesitation, I chose to play the part of Mion.
...Come to think of it, dealing with him might be trickier than dealing with those punks.
``...Well, anyway.@k@vS06/07/151200017. I'm kind of relieved to know you have a girly side after all, Mion.``
``That's a strangely loaded thing to say. What exactly do you mean by that?``
``I was expecting you to pull out some bizarre martial art and take them all down in a flash.@k@vS06/07/151200019. ...So when you actually started crying, I was genuinely surprised.``
``I-I keep telling you!@k@vS06/06/150600334. That was fake! I was acting!@k@vS06/06/150600335. Have you been listening to a single word I've...``
...He placed his large hand gently on top of my head and stroked it, the way you might stroke a baby's.
changeReason: Old says 'stroking a kitten'; new says 'stroking a baby's.' JPN 赤ん坊 means baby, not kitten.

TextENGNew: ...Someone had done this for me when I was little, but@k...it had been so very long since anyone had touched me this way.
It was so... sudden that I... w-was...@k...Why was I getting so flustered...?
...My entire face erupted into flame-red, and the words died in my throat...
``Wai...@k@vS06/06/150600337. ...A-Are you making fun of me...?``
I was blushing and stammering.
But he showed no trace of embarrassment, as cool and natural as if this were the most ordinary thing in the world.
``Ahahahaha.@k@vS06/07/151200021. ...It doesn't matter.@k@vS06/07/151200022. I'm just glad, you know?``
``...Glad? ...About what?``
``That you're safe.``
He said it simply, still stroking my head with that smile of his. ...There was something almost sacred about it, beyond what words could capture.
Our time together was brief.
...I'd been freed from those three, so as far as he was concerned, his mission was already accomplished.
``...Well, I've got to get going.@k@vS06/07/151200025. I'll head off now.``
There were so many first-meeting milestones I wanted to go through with him, but for him, none of those existed.
He had simply rescued a friend in trouble and wanted to leave quickly, without making a big deal of it.
He and Mion were friends. Of course they already knew each other.
...So there was no natural way for me to ask, 'What's your name?'

'Mion's friend,' whose name I didn't even know, smiled at me one more time. His smile was as clear and bright as that high, expansive sky I'd been admiring earlier.@k...With just the faintest hint of mischief.
``Ahaha, relax.@k@vS06/07/151200027. I won't tell anyone that you were crying.``
``...Ahaha, not that anyone would believe me if I did.@k@vS06/07/151200029. Nobody in the world would believe that our peerless class president was in tears.@k@vS06/07/151200030. Ahahahaha.``
He brushed the dust from his clothes one more time and stood up.
Then he walked out of the dim alley and into the dazzling, light-drenched street beyond.
He turned back to look at me.
``Well, see you tomorrow.@k@vS06/07/151200032. At school.``
He dissolved into the blinding sunlight of the main road and was gone.
I stayed where I was, sitting limply in the dim alley.
He had already melted into that world of brilliant light, beyond my sight.
All I could do was sit there, staring blankly into that radiant world, my mind completely empty.
That was how I first met Satoshi-kun...
``That's definitely Satoshi. Satoshi.@k@vS06/03/150300102. That vibe you described, and the habit of casually patting people's heads -- it's gotta be him.``
``...Hmm.@k@vS06/06/150600343. ...So his name is Satoshi-kun...?``
His name was Houjou Satoshi.@kAccording to Mion, he normally had this vague, dreamy quality about him -- like a paper lantern lit in broad daylight.
...So when I told Sis how bravely he'd rushed in to save me, she couldn't quite believe it at first.
``He's usually pretty reliable? ...Well, his little sister's always riding him pretty hard. Ahahaha.``
``Huh?@k@vS06/06/150600345. He has a little sister?``

``Yeah.@k@vS06/03/150300105. Her name's Satoko.@k@vS06/03/150300106. She's a funny kid.@k@vS06/03/150300107. She's got a real devious streak.@k@vS06/03/150300108. But corner her and she bursts into tears.@k@vS06/03/150300109. She's never boring to have around.``
``...Well, things have been rough lately.@k@vS06/03/150300111. ...She's been kind of down.``
When you try to learn about Satoshi-kun, hearing about his sister is inevitable.
...I wanted to absorb everything about his world -- every detail of the life surrounding him.
...Why, though?
Why was I so desperate to dig up every last thing about Satoshi-kun?
...The moment I asked myself that, the sensation from when he'd stroked my head came rushing back.
...I didn't have a mirror, but I could feel the heat in my face.
``Huh? Ahahahaha, sorry!@k@vS06/06/150600347. I'm listening, I'm listening!``

``Well, the thing is, Satoko apparently doesn't get along with her aunt at all...@k@vS06/03/150300115. Satoshi's mature enough to handle it diplomatically, but... Satoko wears her feelings right on her face whenever something upsets her.``
What Sis told me was fascinating.
...First, that both Satoshi-kun and his sister had already lost their parents.
They were now living with their uncle and aunt on their father's side.
And apparently, this uncle and aunt were not exactly people you'd look up to.
Their marriage was deeply hostile, with the husband cheating constantly.
They fought whenever they were in the same room, so the husband apparently spent most of his time at a separate place in Okinomiya.
changeReason: Old says 'He mostly lives with his mistress'; new says 'he spent most of his time at a separate place in Okinomiya.' JPN 興宮の別宅 means separate residence, not mistress's place.

TextENGNew: ...Given all that, Satoshi-kun and Satoko, thrust upon them in the midst of this mess, were never going to be welcomed.
What's more -- and this was truly shocking -- Satoshi-kun's parents had been the Houjou couple, ringleaders of the pro-dam faction.
That would double the resentment.
changeReason: Old vaguely says 'must've added something'; new says 'That would double the resentment.' JPN 憎さも倍増だ explicitly means resentment doubles.

TextENGNew: ...Regardless of where the uncle and aunt had stood during the dam war, they must have suffered considerable collateral damage.

After causing them no end of trouble and then having the nerve to die in an accident, leaving two children behind to be dumped on their doorstep... There would be plenty of hatred, but not a shred of affection.
And this past year or so, the friction between Satoko and the aunt had apparently become especially severe.
When a woman bullies, she does it with insidious cruelty.
It makes no difference if the target is a child.
And she carries a ruthlessness that doesn't care about appearances.
...As a result, Satoko had been ground down relentlessly and was apparently exhausted.
Satoshi-kun might look gentle on the surface, but... I could easily imagine him throwing himself into the line of fire again and again to protect his sister.
Bravely. Just like when he came to save me.
``Then... Satoshi-kun must be exhausted too, right?``
``Every time Satoko gets bullied by their aunt, I bet Satoshi-kun steps in to shield her.@k@vS06/06/150600350. Is it really such a stretch to think he's just as worn out as his sister?``
Had what I said been that sharp?
changeReason: Old says 'Had I said something wrong?'; new says 'Had what I said been that sharp?' JPN そんなにも鋭かったろうか means 'was it that perceptive/sharp?'

TextENGNew: Mion sounded slightly flustered.
Her reaction told me everything.
He always had that vague, dreamy air about him, so he never looked particularly tired.
changeReason: Old says 'Mion can't tell how he's feeling'; new says 'He always had that vague, dreamy air about him, so he never looked particularly tired.' JPN describes his ぽややん (dreamy) demeanor masking his fatigue.

TextENGNew: And so it had never even occurred to her.
How could she be so close to Satoshi-kun every day and still be this oblivious?
I was a little exasperated, but it didn't really matter.
changeReason: Old says 'I was amazed to realize that'; new says 'I was a little exasperated, but it didn't really matter.' JPN 少し呆れるが、まぁそんなことはどうでもいい clearly means exasperation followed by dismissal.

TextENGNew: ``Anyway, forget about that.@k@vS06/06/150600352. So?@k@vS06/06/150600353. What does he usually do?@k@vS06/06/150600354. He looks like the studious type -- does he read a lot?``
``Ah, ahahaha!@k@vS06/03/150300120. Well, just as you'd expect, he mostly spends his time reading.@k@vS06/03/150300121. The book he's reading now is, um... what was it called...?``
After that, I asked about all sorts of things regarding Satoshi-kun.
What he liked, what he disliked, what occupied his thoughts.
I wanted to understand how he thought and what appealed to him, so I asked openly about everything.
This was probably the first time I'd ever been this interested in a specific person.
What the world would call these feelings... I was too embarrassed to acknowledge it, too stubborn to admit it.
So I'm the kind of cheap little doll who falls in love just because someone patted her head...?
changeReason: Old says 'Am I stupid, or what?'; new says 'I'm the kind of cheap little doll who falls in love...' JPN 安っぽいお人形 (cheap doll) is a specific self-deprecating metaphor the old version dropped.

TextENGNew: Whenever I recalled the way his fingers had tickled and tousled the top of my head, my chest went all fluttery.
I tried stroking my own head as an experiment, but I couldn't reproduce that feeling at all.
...Ahahahaha, I'm an idiot.@kWhat am I even doing, petting my own head...?
``Ahahahahahaha...@k@vS06/06/150600356. Sis, I'm done for.@k@vS06/06/150600357. I've completely lost my mind.``
``Huh?@k@vS06/03/150300123. What's gotten into you?@k@vS06/03/150300124. Laughing in that creepy voice all of a sudden...``
``Ahahahahahaha, you couldn't possibly understand how I feel, Sis.@k@vS06/06/150600359. Ahahahahahaha...``
When would I get the chance to see him again?
The life that had felt so suffocating suddenly seemed to burst wide open with light.
